top_entities.name: Vanuatu
top_entities.news_summary: UNGA vote on Vanuatu-led ICJ Advisory Opinion operationalization resolution scheduled for May 20 — tomorrow. Final text affirms binding legal duties on states for climate harm, just transition away from fossil fuels, sea-level-rise legal entitlements, and full reparation duty. Core co-sponsors include Marshall Islands, Micronesia, Palau, Netherlands, Kenya, Singapore, Barbados, Jamaica, Philippines, Sierra Leone. US, backed by oil-producing states, is actively pressuring Vanuatu to withdraw. Vanuatu has refused. A passed resolution is a positive INT-dimension event potentially material enough to affect band proximity (currently 4.1 below Functional floor at 40.0).
top_entities.assessor_note: Pre-vote baseline assessment. Assess TODAY (May 19) to lock in the pre-resolution baseline before the May 20 UNGA vote outcome. Use index composite (35.9) not RS (33.9) as starting baseline. If resolution passes, re-scan May 21 for post-vote assessment. Domestic violence and gender gap remain counterweights to any INT uplift. Never assessed — first-baseline. Staleness: never assessed (25 pts). Volatility: 4.1 below Functional floor (10 pts).

Detroit automaker cluster (GM, Ford, Stellantis) has eliminated 20,000+ US salaried jobs. Laid-off workers describe abrupt termination with scripted HR messages and no empathy. Broader AI-driven workforce restructuring signal across the auto industry. Composite: 40.6 — 0.6 above Functional floor at 40.0.
top_entities.assessor_note: Never assessed. First-baseline assessment. Composite 40.6 is 0.6 above Functional floor — one of the highest volatility situations in the Fortune 500 index. The AI substitution layoffs (500-600 jobs, scripted terminations with no acknowledgment of impact) are a labor-welfare SYS/ACT signal. If first-baseline lands at or below 40.0, a band-crossing proposal to Developing results. The broader Detroit cluster context (20,000+ cuts) is a sector-level SYS signal. Assess carefully — floor proximity is real.

top_entities.name: China
top_entities.news_summary: Xi-Putin summit underway May 19-20 in Beijing. NATO confirmed China supplies 90% of Russia's dual-use technology for Ukraine war. China-Russia described as 'decisive enabler' of Ukraine conflict. Xi reportedly told Trump that Putin may 'regret' Ukraine invasion — furthest Xi has gone in criticizing Russia publicly. Putin arriving days after Trump's Beijing summit. Joint statement expected covering Ukraine, Iran, Taiwan. Xi framing China-Russia ties as 'stabilizing force.' Band-crossing watch: 19.5, 0.5 above Critical floor (20.0). Xi-Putin summit dual-use supplier continuation is a negative BND/INT accumulation event. Assessed May 18.
top_entities.assessor_note: Assessed May 18 (yesterday — band-crossing to Critical applied). New today: Xi-Putin summit is underway with joint statement expected. NATO dual-use supply confirmation (90% figure) and Xi's mild criticism of Putin to Trump are two simultaneous signals in opposite directions (negative: dual-use continuation; weak positive: Xi distancing slightly from full endorsement). Joint statement content will determine whether BND/INT accumulation is reinforced. Flag for conditional reassessment once joint statement text is available (May 20 or 21). Volatility: 0.5 above floor (10 pts).

Transgender health coverage denial suit survived motion to dismiss. Assessed May 17 (2 days ago). Major new discrete events: 8K layoffs executing tomorrow + $375M NM verdict's abatement phase ongoing.

The compound of: child safety verdict ($375M + $3.7B abatement seeking), youth addiction negligence finding, WhatsApp encryption breach class action, and layoff scale with 10% headcount reduction all warrant a conduct documentation update. Assess today to establish pre-layoff-execution baseline; the May 20 layoff event may itself warrant a same-day re-flag.

top_entities.name: OpenAI
top_entities.news_summary: Musk v. Altman trial verdict: May 18, federal jury dismissed ALL of Musk's claims in under 2 hours on statute of limitations grounds. Court did not address breach-of-charitable-trust merits. Musk vowed to appeal to 9th Circuit. HOLD IS LIFTED. OpenAI confirmed at 27.5 per yesterday's close. Verdict is procedurally favorable to OpenAI but does not resolve the underlying governance questions (for-profit conversion, charitable trust integrity). Context: Hundreds of Google/OpenAI employees signed letters backing Anthropic's Pentagon safety stance; OpenAI CEO Altman agreed with Anthropic's red lines.
top_entities.assessor_note: Hold lifted. Verdict (May 18) dismissed all claims on statute of limitations — NOT on merits. This means the for-profit conversion's charitable-trust implications were not ruled on. For scoring: the verdict removes the ongoing litigation risk as a negative INT signal, but the underlying governance structure (hybrid nonprofit/for-profit) and safety policy context remain the core scoring inputs. Last assessed May 5 (14 days ago). Full assessment warranted to incorporate: verdict outcome, safety red-lines stance, Mythos DoD deployment, White House/Pentagon split dynamics, and Altman's agreement with Anthropic safety positions.

Congress active on PBM/insurance reform. 28 days since last assessment.

Assess to determine if floor designation risk persists or if accountability signals have advanced.

top_entities.news_summary: Magyar sworn in as PM May 9 with two-thirds parliamentary majority (138/199 seats). Rule-of-law reforms promised by end of May. EPPO accession committed (EPPO confirmed readiness). EU frozen funds negotiations active (EUR 17B). Magyar said reforms could be in place before end of May. INDEX shows 41.4 Functional — MISMATCH with RS 37.5 Developing. Last assessed May 13. Active forward signals: EU funds plan submission date late May; Sulyok dismissal compliance deadline May 31.
top_entities.assessor_note: CRITICAL BASELINE MISMATCH: INDEX=41.4 (Functional), RS=37.5 (Developing). Assessor must resolve which is canonical before scoring. The Magyar government (sworn in May 9) represents a structural governance change of the highest order — from Orbán's rule-of-law dismantling to a two-thirds reform mandate. EPPO commitment, EU funds engagement, and end-of-May reform deadline are all positive BND/ACT/SYS signals. If RS value (37.5) is current, entity is in Developing approaching Functional. If index (41.4) is current, entity is in Functional and reform trajectory could push toward Established. Assess the May 9 government transition as a major structural change event regardless.
